issue.title: Residual support assets Python sweep U

commands: find bigclaw-go/examples bigclaw-go/docs/reports scripts/ops -type f -name '*.py' 2>/dev/null | sort

blocker.summary: No remaining blocker. The repository-wide physical Python file count was already zero in this workspace before BIG-GO-256 changes, so the lane completed as regression hardening and evidence capture.

notes: This lane records the remaining support assets as already Python-free and ties them to the retained native examples, report artifacts, and helper entrypoints.
The branch baseline was already Python-free, so this lane strengthens issue-scoped regression coverage and validation evidence rather than removing in-branch Python files.
Baseline HEAD before lane changes was `4200cd79`.
The functional lane change landed in `3b2093a2 BIG-GO-256: add support asset python sweep guard`.
Later metadata-only follow-up commits stabilized the issue-local records; use `git log --oneline --grep 'BIG-GO-256'` for the complete branch-local history.
Use `git ls-remote --heads origin big-go-256-land` to read the current pushed head without creating a self-referential metadata loop.
